title: Colombian Military Plane Crashes Near Peru Border, Killing 66
description: A Hercules military transport aircraft went down minutes after takeoff in southern Colombia, leaving 66 dead and 4 missing.
lede: A Colombian military Hercules transport plane crashed minutes after takeoff near the country's southern border with Peru, killing at least 66 people. Four soldiers remain missing as rescue teams search the crash site.

sections.heading: What Happened
paragraphs: The Hercules C-130 military transport aircraft went down shortly after departure from an airbase in southern Colombia, near the border with Peru. Colombian military officials confirmed the crash occurred during daylight hours, with rescue teams immediately dispatched to the scene.

Search and rescue operations are ongoing for four missing soldiers, while recovery efforts continue at the crash site. The aircraft was reportedly carrying military personnel and equipment when it experienced difficulties shortly after takeoff.

sections.heading: Why It Matters
paragraphs: This represents one of the deadliest aviation disasters in recent Colombian military history. The loss of 66 service members deals a significant blow to Colombia's armed forces, which have been actively engaged in counter-narcotics operations and peacekeeping efforts in the region.

The crash occurred in Colombia's strategically important southern border region, an area where military operations are crucial for maintaining security and combating illegal armed groups. The loss of both personnel and equipment could impact ongoing military operations in this sensitive area.

sections.heading: The Bigger Picture
paragraphs: Colombia's military aviation has faced challenges with aging aircraft and demanding operational conditions. The C-130 Hercules, while considered reliable, requires careful maintenance and proper operational protocols, especially in challenging terrain and weather conditions common to Colombia's diverse geography.

sections.heading: What's Next
paragraphs: Colombian authorities have launched a full investigation into the crash, with aviation experts expected to examine weather conditions, mechanical factors, and operational procedures. The search for the four missing soldiers continues as families await news of their loved ones.
